new_classes.label_en: PDO wine (other than Tokaj), in containers > 2 l and ≤ 10 l — other (CN 2204 22 26)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit geschützter Ursprungsbezeichnung (g.U.), kein Tokaj, in Behältnissen von mehr als 2 l bis 10 l — anderer (KN 2204 22 26)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a protected designation of origin (PDO / g.U.) other than Tokaj, presented in containers of more than 2 litres and not exceeding 10 litres, classified under CN code 2204 22 26. Excludes semi-sparkling wine (excess pressure ≥ 1 bar at 20 °C).

coverage_explanation:
The following distinguishing criteria from the legal text and CN hierarchy are captured:
1. Wine of fresh grapes (parent class WineFreshGrapesGrapeMust2204 ✓)
2. Protected designation of origin / g.U. quality (PDOWineQuality restriction ✓)
3. Container size > 2 l and ≤ 10 l (MediumContainer2To10L restriction ✓)
4. Exclusion of Tokaj PDO (complement on TokajOriginQuality ✓)
5. Exclusion of semi-sparkling wine (complement on SemiSparklingWineQuality ✓)
6. Exclusion of sparkling wine (complement on SparklingWineQuality ✓)

Remaining gap (−0.15): The legal note references "Buchstabe a) der Zusätzliche Anmerkung 6 zu Kapitel 22", which defines which wines qualify as PDO wines under this subheading. The specific sub-criteria of Additional Note 6(a) (e.g. actual alcoholic strength ranges and dry extract thresholds) are not yet modelled as structured quality restrictions — they would require dedicated quality classes for alcoholic strength ranges and dry extract, which are partially available (TotalDryExtractQuality exists but no equivalence axiom linking it to a numeric range is present in the running TBox). Additionally, within the 22042222–22042278 block, there may be further sub-distinctions (e.g. actual alcoholic strength ≤ 13%, 13–15%, >15%) that are captured at the TARIC subdivision level but not explicitly distinguishable from the text provided for 22042226 alone.
